paper_title: Field and Laboratory Methods for Peat Bog Sampling and Analysis

methods.description: Process for collecting surface samples from peat bogs to analyze water table gradients and environmental conditions.

steps.description: Surface samples were collected during two field campaigns in the austral summers of January 2012 and 2013.
steps.searchable_summary: collect surface samples from peat bogs

steps.description: Sphagnum capitula were removed, retaining the upper sections of the stems (1-5 cm depth) before storage in airtight plastic bags.
pH and electrical conductivity (EC) were measured in the field adjacent to each sample location.
steps.searchable_summary: measure pH and EC in field

steps.description: WTD was measured relative to the peat bog surface with a metal tape.
Surface samples of 5-10 cm³ were sampled next to each measurement point; these were weighed and dried at 105˚C to calculate moisture contents.

methods.description: Analysis of subsurface samples to investigate differences in amoeba assemblages between surface and subsurface layers.

steps.description: Subsurface samples were taken in 2013 from PAR, SJB and KAR concurrently with surface samples.
steps.searchable_summary: collect subsurface samples from peat bogs

steps.description: Live individuals were not differentiated from empty tests so these samples represent both live individuals of deeper-living species and the communities that lived on the surface in the recent past.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze amoeba assemblages in subsurface samples

methods.description: Evaluation of transfer function models using cross-validation and independent test sets to assess model performance and uncertainty.

steps.description: Included a variety of recently developed tests by cross-validation and using independent test sets.
steps.searchable_summary: perform cross-validation and test set evaluation on transfer function models
